perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the foregoing obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure shall terminate five (5) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that the foregoing obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure shall terminate one (1) year from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the term of confidentiality and non-disclosure from five (5) years to one (1) year. This contradicts the general understanding and potentially other implicit uses of the five-year term elsewhere in the agreement, creating ambiguity about the actual duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dealer agrees and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is the property of CIF, and upon termination of this Agreement all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Inform ation shall be retained exclusively by CIF. In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return any of its Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return all of the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof, within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality term from five years to one year, creating a direct inconsistency. The contradicted text discusses the use and ownership of confidential information upon 'termination of this Agreement.' By changing the duration of confidentiality, it introduces ambiguity and contradiction as to how long the 'Confidential Information' remains confidential. The change alters a key term, which directly impacts other sections of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation and aligns with the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition. The contradicted section makes reference to the term of the agreement, and changing it from 5 years to 1 years directly affects that section by creating a scenario where obligations pertaining to confidential information might not be valid for the initially agreed upon duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not include information that: (i) is in or enters the public domain through no improper action or inaction by Dealer, or (ii) was in Dealers possession or known by Dealer prior to receipt from CIF, or (iii) is rightfully disclosed by Dealer by a third party, or (iv) is approved for disclosure in writing by CIF for release by Dealer or (v) is developed by Dealer without reference to the Confidential Information, or (vi) is required to be disclosed by Dealer in compliance with applicable laws or court order.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not include information that: (i) is in or enters the public domain through no improper action or inaction by Dealer, or (ii) was in Dealers possession or known by Dealer prior to receipt from CIF, or (iii) is rightfully disclosed by Dealer by a third party, or (iv) is approved for disclosure in writing by CIF for release by Dealer or (v) is developed by Dealer with slight reference to the Confidential Information, or (vi) is required to be disclosed by Dealer in compliance with applicable laws or court order.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of what is *not* considered confidential information. Specifically, it alters 'developed by Dealer without reference to the Confidential Information' to 'developed by Dealer with slight reference to the Confidential Information'. This contradicts the original intent of shielding only independently developed information, and the phrase 'slight reference' is open to subjective interpretation. This new definition blurs the line and conflicts with the broader context where Confidential Information is considered protected. Now with this new perturbation is open for debate if something that has slight reference to the Confidential Information, is still under protection or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dealer shall not distribute, disseminate, publish or disclose the Confidential Information to any person, or entity not employed or retained by Dealer or the use of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than to further the marketing and sale of CIF products. Dealer will disclose the Confidential Information only to those individuals employed by Dealer who need to know such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by including information developed with 'slight reference' to it as non-confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits the dealer from disclosing Confidential Information. The phrase 'slight reference' introduces ambiguity, making it unclear whether such information is protected. This creates a strong contradiction because the dealer's obligation not to disclose now has a grey area, potentially leading to disputes over whether information with 'slight reference' is subject to the non-disclosure clause.

perturbation.original_text: Dealer shall not assign this Agreement without the express written permission of CIF.Expect as otherwise provided herein, nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and permitted assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Dealer shall not assign this Agreement with the express written permission of CIF. Expect as otherwise provided herein, this Agreement is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and permitted assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing 'without the express written permission of CIF' to 'with the express written permission of CIF' and removing 'nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is' from 'intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and permitted assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.' The change introduces an internal inconsistency because the original statement explicitly restricted assignment without CIF's permission, while the second implies assignment is allowed with CIF's permission. Also the statement in the original text was meant to mean that there should be no rights for parties outside the present agreement, this now has been removed and is contradictory in nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Binding Effect/Assignment/Benefits . This Agreement shall be binding upon and shal l inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. Dealer shall not assign this Agreement with the express written permission of CIF. Expect as otherwise provided herein, this Agreement is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and permitted assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the contradicted text because it alters the condition for assigning the agreement. Initially, the agreement could not be assigned without CIF's permission. The change makes it possible to assign the agreement WITH CIF's permission. This creates a direct conflict with the original restriction, resulting in an in-text contradiction. Also, the original statement that there should be no rights for parties outside the present agreement, this now has been removed and is contradictory in nature.

perturbation.original_text: Dealer agrees and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is the property of CIF, and upon termination of this Agreement all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Inform ation shall be retained exclusively by CIF. In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return any of its Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return all of the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof, within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof. 2
perturbation.changed_text: Dealer agrees and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is the property of CIF, and upon termination of this Agreement all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Inform ation shall be retained exclusively by CIF. In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return any of its Confidential Information, Dealer is not obligated to return the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof, within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof. 2
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing 'Dealer agrees to return' to 'Dealer is not obligated to return.' This creates an inconsistency because the text first states that Confidential Information is the property of CIF, but then states that the dealer is not obligated to return the confidential information. If it is of exclusive rights to CIF, then the dealer should be obligated to return. Thus, it is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dealer agrees and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is the property of CIF, and upon termination of this Agreement all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Inform ation shall be retained exclusively by CIF. In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return any of its Confidential Information, Dealer is not obligated to return the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof, within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the 'Dealer is not obligated to return the Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the initial statement in both the changed text and the contradicted text: 'Dealer agrees and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is the property of CIF, and upon termination of this Agreement all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Information shall be retained exclusively by CIF.' If CIF has exclusive rights, the dealer should be obligated to return it. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations'.

perturbation.original_text: No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of such party and no failure or delay in enforcing any right hereunder will be deemed a waiver.
perturbation.changed_text: No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made verbally and agreed upon by both parties and no failure or delay in enforcing any right hereunder will be deemed a waiver.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that a waiver or modification of the agreement will be binding if made verbally and agreed upon by both parties. This contradicts the typical requirement for written agreements to modify legal contracts, as it shifts the enforceability from a written document to a verbal agreement. This makes it difficult to enforce the contract as there is no written proof.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Modification . No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made verbally and agreed upon by both parties and no failure or delay in enforcing any right hereunder will be deemed a waiver.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that a waiver or modification of the agreement will be binding if made verbally, contradicting the standard requirement for written agreements. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting an in-text contradiction. The original text clearly stated the necessity for modifications to be 'in writing', but the changed text replaces this with 'verbally and agreed upon by both parties'. This creates a direct conflict, as a verbal agreement is now considered binding, which goes against typical legal practices for contract modifications, making it difficult to enforce and creating an inconsistency within the agreement.
